claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 6 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 6 (under 16)
  - Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£0.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child in hospital for 13 weeks but claimant regularly spends on child
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£0.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
circumstances: Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£0.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
Child is not in local authority care
Child in hospital for 13 weeks but claimant regularly spends on child
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'll help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment process.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! To begin the assessment, I need to know about your child (or children).

**First question: How old is your child?**

The age of your child is important because:
- **Under 16**: May automatically qualify
- **16-19**: May qualify if they're in approved education or training
- **20 or over**: Generally not eligible

Please tell me your child's age. If you have multiple children, we can go through them one at a time.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi, yes I'd like to find out about claiming Child Benefit. I have a child, Alex, who's 6 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! Thank you for that information. Alex is 6 years old, which means they fall into the under-16 category. Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good news so far! Since Alex is 6 years old, they're under 16. Now I need to ask:

**Is Alex working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?**

At 6 years old, Alex is likely not working at all, which is absolutely fine. This question is more relevant for older children, but I need to confirm this for the assessment.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't work at all - they're only 6!

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Of course! That makes perfect sense. Let me continue with the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Next question:

**Is Alex NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right?**

In other words, is Alex receiving benefits like Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) or Universal Credit in their own name? 

Again, at 6 years old, this is very unlikely, but I need to confirm that Alex is not claiming these types of benefits independently.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't receive any benefits in their own name.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! Let me continue to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent! Next question:

**Is Alex in any type of apprenticeship?**

This is another question that's more relevant for older children (typically 16+). At 6 years old, Alex wouldn't be in an apprenticeship. I just need to confirm this to continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in an apprenticeship - they're far too young for that!

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Absolutely right! Let me move forward with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Now we need to check your residency status. This is an important requirement for Child Benefit.

**Do you meet the UK residency requirements?**

You'll qualify if **ANY** of the following applies to you:

1. **You normally live in the UK** - this is the most common situation
2. You have settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. You have pre-settled status and are working (earning above the National Insurance threshold)
4. You have pre-settled status and are actively seeking work (up to 91 days)
5. You have pre-settled status with sufficient resources to support yourself and family
6. You have pre-settled status and are studying with sufficient resources
7. You're a family member of an EEA/Swiss national with the right to reside
8. You're a Crown servant living abroad or living in certain qualifying countries

**Do you normally live in the UK, or does any of these other circumstances apply to you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! Living in the UK meets the residency requirement. Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent! Now I need to confirm your responsibility for Alex.

**Are you responsible for Alex?**

You'll qualify if **ANY** of the following applies:

1. **You live with Alex** - this is the most common situation
2. You contribute to Alex's upkeep by paying at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or equivalent value in things like food, clothes, housing, etc.)
3. You pay maintenance for Alex under a court order or agreement that equals or exceeds the Child Benefit payment

**Do you live with Alex, or do you contribute to their upkeep in one of these ways?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't live with me. I don't currently contribute financially to their upkeep.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I see. This is an important point for eligibility. Let me make sure I understand your situation correctly.

You mentioned that:
- Alex doesn't live with you
- You don't currently contribute financially to their upkeep

**To qualify for Child Benefit, you need to be "responsible" for the child**, which means either:
1. Living with them, OR
2. Contributing to their upkeep (paying at least the equivalent of Child Benefit towards their care - things like food, clothes, housing, pocket money, or presents)

Can I ask a few clarifying questions to make sure I have the full picture?

- **Where does Alex live?** (For example, with another parent, a relative, or in care?)
- **Do you have any contact or involvement with Alex?**
- **Are there any plans for you to start contributing to Alex's upkeep or for Alex to live with you?**

This will help me determine your eligibility accurately.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex doesn't live with me - they live with my ex-partner. I don't have any financial arrangements in place at the moment. I'm just trying to understand if I could claim Child Benefit.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. I understand your situation now.

Based on what you've told me, since Alex lives with your ex-partner and you don't currently have any financial arrangements in place to contribute to Alex's upkeep, you would not meet the "responsibility" requirement for Child Benefit.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Assessment Result: Not Eligible

I'm sorry, but based on your circumstances, **you are not currently eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex**.

### Why you're not eligible:

To claim Child Benefit, you must be **"responsible" for the child**. This means you need to either:
- **Live with the child**, OR
- **Contribute to their upkeep** by paying at least the equivalent of the Child Benefit amount towards their care (such as food, clothes, housing, pocket money, presents, or maintenance payments)

Since Alex lives with your ex-partner and you don't currently have any financial arrangements in place to contribute to Alex's upkeep, you don't meet this responsibility requirement.

### What you could consider:

1. **If you're planning to set up maintenance payments** or start contributing to Alex's care costs, you could become eligible once those arrangements are in place - as long as your contributions equal or exceed the Child Benefit amount.

2. **Your ex-partner may be eligible** - If they live with Alex and are responsible for their care, they should be able to claim Child Benefit. Only one person can claim Child Benefit for a child at any time.

3. **If you have a formal maintenance agreement** in the future, you may want to revisit your eligibility at that point.

If you have any questions or if your circumstances change, please feel free to reach out again.

reasonings: Alex is 6 years old (under 16), not working, not receiving benefits in their own right, and not in an apprenticeship. The claimant meets UK residency requirements (living in Manchester). However, the claimant does not meet the 'responsibility' requirement because: (1) Alex does not live with the claimant - Alex lives with the claimant's ex-partner, and (2) the claimant does not contribute financially to Alex's upkeep. To qualify for Child Benefit, the claimant must be 'responsible' for the child, which requires either living with the child OR contributing to their upkeep by paying at least the equivalent of Child Benefit towards their care. Since neither condition is met, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex because they do not meet the responsibility requirement. While Alex qualifies as a child and the claimant meets residency requirements, the claimant neither lives with Alex nor contributes to Alex's upkeep. Child Benefit can only be claimed by someone who is responsible for the child.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national

Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

Contributions include: food, clothes, pocket money, housing, presents
Can be weekly, monthly, or lump sum payments

Amount equals or exceeds Child Benefit payment

response.guidance: You must be responsible for the child, which means either living with them OR paying at least the Child Benefit amount towards their care (food, clothes, housing, pocket money, etc.). This can include maintenance payments under court order.
